methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
BlackSea Technologies, a platform-stage company in Defense & Security backed by Razor's Edge Ventures, sits within a sector encompassing 19 peer organizations—2 public and 8 private entities identified. Top private comparables include Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems; public benchmarks are represented by DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable to this valuation. A search for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category, below the minimum threshold for reliable median derivation. The broader Defense & Security universe of 19 firms does not compress into a sufficiently narrow cohort to anchor valuation off peer multiples without introducing material distortion. Applying an all-sector median would conflate heterogeneous business models, geographies, and technology maturity levels.

Without triangulated peer analysis, valuation derivation requires alternative methods—such as discounted cash flow, comparable transaction precedents, or venture capital method calibration—none of which are present in the available dataset. A credible range cannot be determined from the provided inputs. Immediate uncertainties include the company's revenue profile, customer concentration, and capital efficiency metrics relative to the identified peer set. Resolution of these gaps is necessary before assigning a defensible valuation.
